title: excoecaria colliguaya
intro: Excoecaria colliguaya, also known as the sandpaper tree or white mangrove, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Indo-Pacific area. The medicinal properties of Excoecaria colliguaya are attributed to its leaves, which contain a toxic compound called phorbol, but are also known for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, wounds, and fungal infections, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Excoecaria colliguaya can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
